policy.title: RBI releases final guidelines on Climate Risk and Sustainable Finance
policy.description: RBI mandates scheduled commercial banks to integrate climate scenario analysis into ICAAP by March 2027, with interim reporting requirements starting September 2026. Guidelines specify physical risk assessment must cover at least 70% of loan book exposure by value.

policy.title: SEBI expands BRSR Core to include Scope 3 emissions for top 500 listed companies
policy.description: SEBI circular mandates Scope 3 GHG reporting and water stress assessments under BRSR Core from FY 2026-27. Third-party assurance now required for all climate-related metrics, not just reasonable assurance for select indicators.
policy.source: Securities and Exchange Board of India

policy.title: IRDAI issues exposure draft on Parametric Insurance Products Framework
policy.description: Draft framework permits banks and insurers to co-develop parametric products for agriculture, SME, and MSME segments tied to IMD weather triggers. Public comments invited until 30 April 2026, with final rules expected by Q3 2026.
policy.source: Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India

policy.title: Basel Committee finalizes Pillar 3 climate risk disclosure framework
policy.description: Framework requires G-SIBs to disclose transition risk exposures by sector and physical risk by geography starting January 2027. Includes standardized metrics for financed emissions intensity and climate-related credit losses.

policy.title: NGFS releases Phase V climate scenarios with enhanced physical risk pathways
policy.description: Updated scenarios incorporate latest IPCC AR6 damage functions and extend chronic physical risk modeling to 2100. New 'Current Policies Hot House' scenario projects 3.2°C warming with acute physical losses 40% higher than Phase IV estimates.
policy.source: Network for Greening the Financial System

policy.title: EFRAG publishes final sector-specific ESRS for financial institutions
policy.description: Standards require banks to disclose physical and transition risk exposures at counterparty level and alignment of lending portfolios with EU Taxonomy. Effective for FY 2026 reporting for large EU-listed financial institutions.

competitors.title: Jupiter Intelligence closes $115M Series D, launches sovereign risk module
competitors.description: Funding led by TPG Rise Climate to expand emerging markets coverage. New ClimateScore Sovereign product provides country-level physical risk ratings targeting development finance institutions and multilateral lenders.

competitors.title: GIST Advisory partners with SIDBI for MSME climate risk assessment pilot
competitors.description: Partnership will deploy GIST's natural capital valuation tools across 5,000 MSMEs in textile and agri-processing clusters. Pilot aims to integrate environmental risk scores into SIDBI's credit appraisal by Q4 2026.

competitors.title: Moody's launches Physical Climate Risk Scores for Indian corporate bonds
competitors.description: New dataset covers 800+ Indian corporate issuers with asset-level flood, cyclone, and heat stress scores. Product integrates with Moody's credit ratings platform for fixed income portfolio analytics.

competitors.title: Sprih raises $8M Series A to expand BRSR compliance platform
competitors.description: Round led by Omnivore with participation from Better Capital. Funds earmarked for AI-powered data collection tools targeting mid-cap companies facing new SEBI BRSR Core mandates.

competitors.title: XDI signs multi-year agreement with Asian Development Bank
competitors.description: Agreement provides ADB with asset-level physical risk analytics for infrastructure project screening across South and Southeast Asia. XDI will deliver risk assessments for ADB's $5B climate adaptation pipeline.

competitors.title: Watershed launches financial services module with loan-level emissions tracking
competitors.description: New product enables banks to calculate financed emissions at individual loan level with PCAF-compliant methodology. Initial clients include three European universal banks seeking CSRD compliance.

research.title: Physical Climate Risk in Indian Bank Portfolios: A Stress Testing Framework
research.description: RBI study finds 12-18% of PSU bank agricultural loan portfolios face high physical risk exposure, primarily from monsoon variability and heat stress. Recommends differentiated provisioning based on district-level hazard scores.

research.title: Scaling Parametric Insurance in South Asia: Lessons and Roadmap
research.description: IFC analysis shows parametric products could address $8B annual protection gap for climate perils in India. Report identifies trigger design and basis risk as primary barriers, recommending hybrid index-indemnity structures for SME segments.

research.title: Climate-Adjusted Probability of Default Models: Methodological Guide
research.description: UNEPFI guide provides technical specifications for integrating physical hazard data into PD models, with case studies from six pilot banks. Finds climate adjustment can shift PD estimates by 50-200 basis points for high-exposure sectors.

research.title: ESG Data Gaps in Emerging Markets: Investor Survey 2026
research.description: Survey of 120 institutional investors finds 78% cite data availability as primary barrier to EM climate investments. Physical risk data and Scope 3 emissions identified as most critical gaps, with India ranked 4th globally for ESG data needs.

research.title: sigma 1/2026: Natural catastrophes in 2025 — economic and insured losses
research.description: Global insured losses from natural catastrophes reached $148B in 2025, with Asia-Pacific accounting for 28%. India saw record $4.2B insured losses from monsoon flooding, a 65% increase from 2024.

research.title: Climate Risk Insurance for Agriculture: Global Stocktake
research.description: Report documents 340M smallholders globally now covered by some form of climate risk insurance, up from 280M in 2023. India's PMFBY remains largest single program but penetration in non-cereal crops under 15%.

ourRead.headline: RBI's March 2027 deadline creates an 18-month land grab for climate risk analytics providers — StepChange must secure anchor bank partnerships before global players localize.
body: This week's intelligence confirms a decisive shift: Indian climate risk regulation has moved from 'guidance' to 'mandate' with concrete timelines. The RBI's final climate risk framework, SEBI's expanded BRSR Core requirements, and IRDAI's parametric insurance draft together signal that 2026-27 will be the year Indian financial institutions must operationalize climate analytics, not merely discuss them.

The RBI's requirement for climate scenario analysis in ICAAP by March 2027, covering 70% of loan book exposure, is the most significant policy development for StepChange in the past year. This is not a voluntary exercise — banks will need hazard-level data mapped to borrower locations, PD/LGD adjustment methodologies, and audit-ready documentation. The RBI's own research paper identifying 12-18% of PSU agricultural portfolios as high-risk provides the empirical foundation StepChange needs for client conversations. We should immediately benchmark our district-level hazard coverage against this threshold.

The competitor landscape is accelerating faster than anticipated. Jupiter's $115M raise and sovereign risk module signals aggressive emerging market expansion; their TPG Rise Climate backing gives them credibility with DFIs that could cascade to Indian banks. More immediately concerning is GIST Advisory's SIDBI partnership — this positions a domestic competitor in the MSME credit assessment space where StepChange should own the conversation. Moody's India-specific physical risk scores for 800+ corporates is a flanking move we must counter with superior granularity and local hazard calibration.

The UNEPFI climate-adjusted PD guide is a gift for our sales engineering team — it validates our core methodology and provides a common language for bank conversations. The finding that climate adjustment can shift PD estimates by 50-200 basis points for high-exposure sectors is precisely the materiality threshold that will move credit committees. We should build a quick-start implementation guide mapping UNEPFI methodology to StepChange platform capabilities.

Watch IRDAI's parametric framework closely — the April 30 comment deadline means final rules by Q3, and first-mover banks will define product structures. One action this week: schedule conversations with three PSU banks about their March 2027 ICAAP readiness, using the RBI's own 12-18% agricultural exposure finding as the door-opener.
